claim: AI agents running on coding tasks now perform measurably better on multi-step agentic benchmarks compared to a year ago, with progress visible in real-world knowledge work tasks like document analysis and synthesis
actionable: Use recent frontier models (Opus 4.8, GPT 5.5) for agentic workflows; benchmark your specific tasks against published datasets to verify capability gains.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Model inference costs per unit of intelligence fall 10-100x every 6-18 months as older capability tiers become available in cheaper models; smart model selection can reduce cost per task by orders of magnitude
actionable: Periodically audit your inference provider contracts; benchmark cheaper recent models against your previous choices to identify 10x cost reduction opportunities.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Agentic loops dramatically increase token consumption because agents retry, verify work, and iterate across multiple turns—20-100 turns per task is common in knowledge work, acting as a token multiplier on costs
actionable: When evaluating agentic systems, multiply base token costs by estimated turn count per task; consider deterministic workflows (regex, state machines) for predictable tasks to avoid token bloat.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Reasoning models increase output token volume significantly (3x more tokens observed) but may not always translate to better outcomes for non-frontier tasks; reasoning cost must be justified against task complexity
actionable: Test reasoning models on your actual use cases before switching; compare cost-weighted quality on real outputs, not benchmark scores alone.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Multi-model architectures that allow switching between providers (vs. vendor lock-in) protect businesses from sudden price increases, model deprecation, and capability shifts
actionable: Architect products to support multiple inference providers; implement model abstraction layers so you can swap providers without breaking product code.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Notion discovered that harness engineering and architecture decisions can account for ~3x the cost delta as pure model selection; efficient prompt patterns and caching often yield bigger wins than model upgrades
actionable: Before switching models, audit your prompting patterns, use prompt caching, implement request batching, and optimize your harness—expect 3x more impact than model swaps.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Many routine tasks (form filling, CSV processing, API integration) do not require LLM reasoning; deterministic CPU-based workflows can reduce token costs by 80% on repeated, well-defined tasks
actionable: For deterministic tasks, build state machines and worker functions that call APIs directly instead of querying an LLM every iteration.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Software development tasks are now commoditized; basic coding and feature implementation are no longer gatekept skills, shifting value from execution to ideation and product strategy
actionable: As an engineer, focus on understanding business problems, customer needs, and architectural decisions—not on coding speed; invest time in learning how to use AI tools well rather than tool mastery.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: The primary function of long-running coding agents is to iteratively debug and verify their own work across 48+ hour sessions, but they suffer from memory drift and context loss after 10-15 turns unless actively managed with persistent state
actionable: For long-horizon coding tasks, implement explicit memory checkpoints after every 5-10 turns; store work state to disk and reload context to prevent agent drift.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Voice agent latency budgets are extremely tight; Python SDKs introduce unacceptable latency for real-time voice interactions—switching to Rust-based implementations is necessary for sub-100ms response times in production voice systems
actionable: If building production voice agents, implement the hotpath in a compiled language (Rust, Go, C++) and reserve Python for non-latency-critical orchestration.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Full-duplex voice systems require state machines and regex-based intent matching rather than LLM-per-turn models to achieve latency and cost targets; deterministic control flow is essential for voice agents at scale
actionable: Design voice agents with regex patterns for common intents (confirmation, repetition, numerical input) and reserve LLM calls for fallback/clarification paths only.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Open-weight models trail frontier models by 3-9 months in capability but remain viable for most production tasks; open models will continue closing the gap if labs maintain incentive structures for publishing weights
actionable: Evaluate open-weight models monthly for cost-critical tasks; benchmark against your baseline task (e.g., task that required Opus 4.5 six months ago may now work with open-weight).
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1

claim: Senior engineers must now understand agents fundamentally—being able to explain agentic loops, tool invocation, and retry logic on a whiteboard is becoming a baseline hiring bar alongside database design
actionable: Build a simple agent (control loop + tool calls) from scratch in 300 lines; understand the pattern deeply enough to explain it to a junior engineer.
source_title: AI Engineer Melbourne 2026 Keynote Livestream | Day 1
